item.description: Hi there! I am a Ph.D student in theoretical physics at the University of British Columbia, Canada. I am interested in all aspects of quantum field theory - formal, observational, and applications in other fields (e.g. modeling deep neural networks). I did my Bachelor’s in Electrical Engineering at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology, Bangladesh. I will be at Amplitudes 2024 Conference, IAS, Princeton, New Jersey, June 10- June 14, 2024 Institut des Hautes Études Scientifiques (IHES), Paris, June 17-August 7, 2024 I was at Strings 2023, Perimeter Institute, Canada, July 24-29, 2023 TASI summer school, University of Colorado, Boulder, USA, June 5-30, 2024 If our worldlines cross at any of the above places, feel free to shoot me an email.

I represented Bangladesh in the International Physics Olympiads (IPhO) in Estonia (2012) and Denmark (2013). Later, I was involved in the training and team selection of the Bangladesh National Team participating in IPhO for 2014-2016. Since 2022, I am involved in the writing of The Canadian Association of Physics exam and the selection of Canadian team to participate in IPhO. Some problems I wrote for those exams can be found at

For my most up-to-date academic profile, please visit inspire. The BCFT Spectrum from AdS Brane Mergers arxiv:2209.11227 Biswas, S., Kastikainen, J., Sashi, S., Sully, J. JHEP 11 (2022) 158. Massless Fermions on a half-space: The curious case of 2+1-dimensions arXiv:2208.06374 Biswas, S., Semenoff, G. W., JHEP 10 (2022) 045 Soft Scalars don’t decouple arXiv:2208.05023 Biswas, S., Semenoff, G. W., J Phys. Rev. D (2022) 106, 105023 The spectrum of boundary states in symmetric orbifolds arXiv:2110.

item.title: Contact
item.description: shovon432@gmail.com Room 418, Hennings Building 6224 Agricultural Road, Vancouver, British Columbia V6T 1Z1, Canada.\ Linkedin: Shovon Biswas
